heading: Transportation of United States Government personnel
label: 55302 Transportation of United States Government personnel
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)5530246 App.:1241(a)., title IX, § 901(a), ; , ; , , .June 29, 1936, ch. 85849 Stat. 2015Aug. 26, 1954, ch. 93668 Stat. 832Pub. L. 104–316, title I, § 125110 Stat. 3839Oct. 19, 1996In subsection (a), the words “by sea” are added for clarity. The words “a territory or possession of the United States” are substituted for “any of the possessions of the United States” for consistency in the revised title.

L. 104–316, title I, § 125110 Stat. 3839Oct. 19, 1996In subsection (a), the words “by sea” are added for clarity. The words “a territory or possession of the United States” are substituted for “any of the possessions of the United States” for consistency in the revised title.

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: An officer or employee of the United States Government traveling by sea on official business overseas or to or from a territory or possession of the United States shall travel and transport personal effects on a vessel documented under the laws of the United Sates if such a vessel is available, unless the necessity of the mission requires the use of a foreign vessel.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: b .—Regulations
children.text: The Administrator of General Services shall prescribe regulations under which agencies may not pay for or reimburse an officer or employee for travel or transportation expenses incurred on a foreign vessel in the absence of satisfactory proof of the necessity of using the vessel.
